nameMeanings.name: Henricsson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of Swedish patronymic Henriksson (see Henrikson ) or of its Danish, Norwegian, and North German cognates (see Henricksen ). Compare Hendrickson .

variants.name: Henrikson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Bertel, Lars, Sig, Thor.

Americanized form (and a rare Swedish variant) of Swedish Henriksson: patronymic from the personal name Henrik (see Henry 1). This is also an Americanized form of the Norwegian and Danish cognate Henriksen . Compare Hendrickson and Henrickson .

nameMeanings.name: Jap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1 Short form of any of the given names beginning with the letter J- ( compare Dee and Kay ), now also used as an independent given name.
2  Indian male name meaning ‘victory’ in Sanskrit.

variants.name: Jaye
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jay  , used mainly for girls.

nameMeanings.name: Jacob
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, from Hebrew Yaakov. This was borne by perhaps the most important of all the patriarchs in the Book of Genesis. Jacob was the father of twelve sons, who gave their names to the twelve tribes of Israel. He was the son of Isaac and Rebecca and twin brother of Esau. According to the story in Genesis, he was the cunning younger twin, who persuaded his brother Esau to part with his right to his inheritance in exchange for a bowl of soup (‘a mess of pottage’). Later, he tricked his blind and dying father into blessing him in place of Esau. The derivation of the name has been much discussed. It is traditionally explained as being derived from Hebrew akev ‘heel’ and to have meant ‘heel grabber’, because when Jacob was born ‘his hand took hold of Esau's heel’ (Genesis 25:26). This is interpreted later in the Bible as ‘supplanter’ Esau himself remarks, ‘Is he not rightly named Jacob? for he has supplanted me these two times’ (Genesis 27:36). Jacob is especially common as a Jewish given name, although it also became very popular among the Puritans from the 16th century onwards, and has again been widely used since the 1990s. Compare James .

historicEvents.title: The Russo-Swedish War
historicEvents.description: While in an alliance with Grand Prince Ivan III of Moscow, King Hans of Denmark and Norway invaded Sweden and was declared King of Sweden.

historicEvents.title: Swedish War of Liberation
historicEvents.description: The Swedish War of Liberation was a civil war led by nobleman Gustav Vasa. As Christian II heard of the rebellion, he confronted Vasa's men, who won the battle at Brunbacks Ferry. Christian II was defeated by the Swedes in Stockholm and a truce was agreed upon. Vasa was given as a hostage as a show of good faith until the treaty was signed. The Danish King did not keep his word and Vasa was held as a prisoner for two years until his escape. A bounty was placed on Vasa's head. Vasa worked to inspire many to join his cause and lived under disguise until the Danes surrendered in 1525.

historicEvents.title: Treaty of Malmo
historicEvents.description: The Treaty of Malmo was signed on September 1, 1524, which ended the Swedish War of Liberation and the independence of Sweden was recognized by Denmark and Norway.
Peace of Stettin concluded. Denmark recognized independence of Sweden. Sweden gave up claim to Norway.

en.lifeSummary: Jap Jacob Henricsson was born in Karlborn, Dalarna, Sweden as the son of Henric Simla and Kjerstin. He married Gudlög Hansdotter Svinhuvud in 1510, in Sundborn, Kopparberg, Sweden. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Jap Jacob Henricsson est né à Karlborn, Dalarna, Suède, fils de Henric Simla et Kjerstin. Il a épousé Gudlög Hansdotter Svinhuvud en 1510, à Sundborn, Kopparberg, Suède. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Jap Jacob Henricsson wurde in Karlborn, Dalarna, Schweden, als Sohn von Henric Simla und Kjerstin geboren. Er hat 1510 in Sundborn, Kopparberg, Schweden Gudlög Hansdotter Svinhuvud geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Jap Jacob Henricsson è nato il nel Karlborn, Dalarna, Svezia come figlio di Henric Simla e Kjerstin. Lui sposato Gudlög Hansdotter Svinhuvud nel 1510, nel Sundborn, Kopparberg, Svezia. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 1 figlia.

ja.lifeSummary: Jap Jacob HenricssonはスウェーデンダラーナカルルボルンでHenric SimlaとKjerstinの息子として生まれた。1510年にスウェーデンコッパルバーグスンドボルンでGudlög Hansdotter Svinhuvudと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Jap Jacob Henricsson(은)는 스웨덴 다라른아 칼본에서 Henric Simla(와)과 Kjerstin의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1510년 스웨덴 코파르버그 선드본에서 Gudlög Hansdotter Svinhuvud(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Jap Jacob Henricsson nasceu em Karlborn, Dalarna, Suécia filho de Henric Simla e Kjerstin. Ele casou-se com Gudlög Hansdotter Svinhuvud em 1510, em Sundborn, Kopparberg, Suécia. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Jap Jacob Henricsson родился в местности карлборн, даларна, Швеция. Его родителей звали Henric Simla и Kjerstin. Он женился на женщине по имени Gudlög Hansdotter Svinhuvud в 1510 г. в местности сундборн, коппарбирг, Швеция. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Jap Jacob Henricsson nació en Karlborn, Dalarna, Suecia como hijo de Henric Simla y Kjerstin. Se casó con Gudlög Hansdotter Svinhuvud en 1510, en Sundborn, Kopparberg, Suecia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 1 hija.
